name: Disable Remote Desktop (RDP)
description: Prevents inbound RDP connections. Closes port 3389. Recommended unless you actively use RDP to connect to this machine.

name: Disable Remote Assistance
description: Prevents anyone from remotely controlling this machine via Remote Assistance (msra.exe).

name: Set UAC to Maximum Level
description: Enables the highest UAC prompt level — always prompt, even for built-in admins. Best practice on any workstation.

name: Disable LLMNR (Link-Local Multicast Name Resolution)
description: LLMNR is exploited by MITM attacks (Responder tool). Disable unless you're on a legacy network that requires it.

name: Disable NetBIOS over TCP/IP (via policy)
description: Disables NetBIOS name resolution. Removes NBT-NS, which is used in Responder/MITM attacks on local networks.

name: Ensure Windows Firewall is Enabled (All Profiles)
description: Enables Windows Firewall for Domain, Private, and Public profiles. Safe default — should never be disabled.

name: Disable SMBv1 Protocol
description: Disables the legacy SMBv1 protocol exploited by WannaCry/EternalBlue. Safe on any modern Windows — SMBv2/3 handles all modern file sharing.

name: Set PowerShell Execution Policy to RemoteSigned
description: Requires downloaded scripts to be signed by a trusted publisher. Allows local scripts. Balanced security without breaking automation.

name: Enable PowerShell Script Block Logging
description: Logs all PowerShell script blocks to the Windows event log. Invaluable for detecting malicious scripts. Slight overhead on PS-heavy systems.

name: Disable WPAD Auto-Proxy Discovery
description: Prevents Windows from auto-discovering proxy settings via WPAD. WPAD is exploited for MITM proxy injection attacks on untrusted networks.

name: Disable Office Macros (if Office installed)
description: Blocks all VBA macros in Office documents except those digitally signed. #1 attack vector for corporate malware. No impact if you never use macros.

name: Harden Accessibility Feature Backdoor (Sticky Keys)
description: Replaces the Sticky Keys / Ease of Access shortcut executables with a disabled flag. These are abused to get a SYSTEM shell from the login screen.

name: Disable AutoRun on All Drive Types
description: Prevents AutoRun from executing content on USB drives, CDs, DVDs, and network shares. Blocks a classic malware delivery vector.

name: Restrict Anonymous Network Access (Null Sessions)
description: Prevents unauthenticated users from enumerating shares, user accounts, and SAM data over the network. Hardens against network reconnaissance attacks.

name: Require Ctrl+Alt+Del at Login
description: Forces the secure attention sequence before the login prompt. Prevents spoofed login screens from harvesting credentials.

name: Clear Pagefile on Shutdown
description: Zeros the pagefile during a clean shutdown. Prevents sensitive data (passwords, keys, tokens) from being recovered from the pagefile after shutdown. Adds ~30 seconds to shutdown time.

name: Require SMB Signing on Workstation
description: Mandates cryptographic signing on all outbound SMB connections. Blocks SMB relay attacks where an attacker intercepts and replays authentication tokens on the local network.

name: Enable Windows Defender Credential Guard
description: Enables Credential Guard to isolate the Local Security Authority (LSASS) process using Virtualisation-Based Security. Prevents credential theft via memory dumping attacks.

name: Disable Remote Scheduled Tasks (ATS)
description: Disables the Remote Registry server task. Prevents remote machines from querying the registry of this PC.

name: Enable Exploit Protection (Memory Protection)
description: Enables all Windows Defender Exploit Protection memory mitigations: Control Flow Guard, Data Execution Prevention, and Randomised Memory Layouts.

name: Disable Administrative Shares
description: Prevents Windows from creating default admin shares (C$, ADMIN$) on boot. Reduces the number of exposed network endpoints on domain-joined machines.

name: Require Network Level Authentication (NLA) for RDP
description: Requires the user to authenticate before a remote desktop session is established. Prevents DoS attacks that exhaust server resources with unauthenticated connections.

name: Harden Print Spooler RPC Interface
description: Configures the Print Spooler to only accept authenticated connections on the RPC interface. Mitigates PrintNightmare and other Spooler exploits.

steps.cmd: reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers" /v RegisterSpoolerFileQuotedPaths /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f 2>nul || echo Already set or not applicable

name: Disable LAN Manager (LM) Authentication
description: Prevents the use of the legacy LAN Manager authentication protocol which uses weak DES keys. Modern NTLMv2 is always used when LM is disabled.

name: Enable Force ASLR
description: Forces all executables to use Address Space Layout Randomisation even if they were not compiled with /DYNAMICBASE. Closes a gap exploited by return-oriented programming attacks.

name: Disable IPv6 Router Discovery
description: Prevents the machine from accepting router advertisements. IPv6 routing is disabled unless manually configured — useful for isolating the machine on IPv6 networks.

name: Enable Windows Defender Hardening
description: Enables all Defender policy-based hardening: real-time behaviour monitoring, script scanning, and cloud-delivered protection.

name: Disable SMBv1 Client Driver
description: Unloads the SMBv1 client driver (mrxdaf.sys) so this PC cannot be used as a conduit to spread SMB exploits to other machines.

name: Disable UAC Elevation on Remote Connections
description: Prevents programs from automatically elevating when launched via a remote desktop session. Closes an elevation attack vector in RDP scenarios.

name: Enable SmartScreen Filter (All Users)
description: Enables Windows SmartScreen for all users via policy — checks downloaded files and web content against a Microsoft reputation database.

name: Disable Loading Test-Signed Drivers
description: Prevents Windows from loading drivers signed with test certificates. Only production-signed drivers will load — blocks rootkit-style drivers.

name: Require SMB Encryption on All Shares
description: Requires SMB encryption for all file shares — prevents any unencrypted SMB connection. Clients must support SMB3.

name: Disable Admin Approval Mode Bypass for COM
description: Prevents COM objects from bypassing the Admin Approval Mode UAC prompt.

name: Disable Credential Prompt for WSH Scripts
description: Requires WSH scripts to run without elevated privileges unless explicitly requested.

name: Disable Office File Validation for Faster Loading
description: Disables the Office File Validation inspector that scans downloaded files. Minor security reduction but faster file opening.

name: Enable Windows Defender Heap Protection
description: Enables all Windows Defender exploit protection heap mitigations.

name: Disable WDigest Credential Caching
description: Prevents Windows from caching credentials in memory for WDigest authentication. Mitigates credential theft attacks.

name: Enable LSASS Protected Process
description: Enables LSASS to run as a protected process, preventing tools from dumping credentials from memory.

name: Disable Anonymous Enumeration of Shares and SAM
description: Prevents anonymous users from listing shares and enumerating SAM accounts over the network.

name: Disable Office OLE Object Embedding
description: Prevents Office documents from embedding and automatically updating linked OLE objects. Blocks an exploit vector used in document-based malware.

name: Ensure Secure Boot is Enabled
description: Verifies that Secure Boot is enabled in UEFI firmware. Displays a warning if it is not.

name: Disable AutoRun for Executable Media
description: Completely disables AutoRun for all drive types, preventing malware auto-execution from USB and network drives.

name: Enable BitLocker with TPM Only
description: Configures BitLocker to use TPM-only authentication (no recovery key required). Best security for machines with TPM.

name: Harden Windows Security Center Notifications
description: Configures Windows Security Center to suppress low-priority notifications while keeping real-time protection active.

name: Enable Process Creation Auditing
description: Enables detailed process creation event logging in the Windows Security event log.
